quiz.title: Unit 4 Quiz 11, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Electron Geometry and Regions of Electron Density
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: VSEPR Theory, Electron Geometry, Regions of Electron Density, Lone Pairs in Electron Geometry, Molecular vs Electron Geometry

questions.question: What is the electron geometry of CH₄?
questions.explanation: CH₄ has four bonding pairs and no lone pairs around carbon, giving it a tetrahedral electron geometry with bond angles of 109.5°.

questions.question: The electron geometry of NH₃ is:
questions.explanation: NH₃ has three bonding pairs and one lone pair around nitrogen, giving it four regions of electron density. Therefore, its electron geometry is tetrahedral, even though its molecular shape is trigonal pyramidal.

questions.question: H₂O has how many electron regions around oxygen?
questions.explanation: Oxygen in H₂O has two bonding pairs and two lone pairs, giving it four regions of electron density. This makes its electron geometry tetrahedral, even though its molecular shape is bent.

questions.question: Which molecule has octahedral electron geometry?
questions.explanation: SF₆ (sulfur hexafluoride) has octahedral electron geometry. Sulfur has six bonding pairs and no lone pairs, giving it six regions of electron density arranged octahedrally with 90° bond angles. CH₄ is tetrahedral, PCl₃ is tetrahedral (electron geometry) with trigonal pyramidal molecular shape, and NH₃ is tetrahedral (electron geometry) with trigonal pyramidal molecular shape.

questions.question: The electron geometry of CO₂ is:
questions.explanation: CO₂ has two double bonds (each counted as one region) and no lone pairs on carbon, giving it two regions of electron density. This results in a linear electron geometry with 180° bond angles.

questions.question: Which molecule has trigonal planar electron geometry?
questions.explanation: BF₃ has three bonding pairs and no lone pairs on boron, giving it three regions of electron density and trigonal planar electron geometry (120° bond angles).

questions.question: Electron geometry counts lone pairs as:
options: Twice a region of electron density
Half a region of electron density
questions.explanation: In VSEPR theory, each lone pair counts as one region of electron density, just like bonding pairs. Both lone pairs and bonding pairs contribute to the electron geometry.

questions.question: What is the electron geometry of PCl₃?
questions.explanation: PCl₃ has three bonding pairs and one lone pair on phosphorus, giving it four regions of electron density. Its electron geometry is tetrahedral, while its molecular shape is trigonal pyramidal.

questions.question: SF₆ has what electron geometry?
questions.explanation: SF₆ has six bonding pairs and no lone pairs on sulfur, giving it six regions of electron density. This results in an octahedral electron geometry with 90° bond angles.

questions.question: The electron geometry of NO₃⁻ is:
questions.explanation: NO₃⁻ (nitrate ion) has three regions of electron density around nitrogen (resonance structures mean all bonds are equivalent) and no lone pairs, giving it trigonal planar electron geometry.

questions.question: NH₃ has how many regions of electron density?
questions.explanation: NH₃ has three bonding pairs and one lone pair around nitrogen, giving it a total of four regions of electron density.

questions.question: H₂O electron geometry is:
questions.explanation: H₂O has four regions of electron density (two bonding pairs and two lone pairs), so its electron geometry is tetrahedral. The molecular shape (bent) is different.

questions.question: The electron geometry of PCl₅ is:
questions.explanation: PCl₅ has five bonding pairs and no lone pairs on phosphorus, giving it five regions of electron density. This results in a trigonal bipyramidal electron geometry.

questions.question: XeF₄ has how many electron regions around Xe?
questions.explanation: XeF₄ has four bonding pairs and two lone pairs around xenon, giving it a total of six regions of electron density. This results in an octahedral electron geometry with a square planar molecular shape.

questions.question: Electron geometry of SO₂ is:
questions.explanation: SO₂ has two bonding pairs (one double bond and one single bond in resonance structures) and one lone pair on sulfur, giving it three regions of electron density. Therefore, its electron geometry is trigonal planar, while its molecular shape is bent.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered electron geometry and VSEPR theory! 🧪📐
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the difference between electron geometry and molecular shape.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on counting regions of electron density correctly.
scoreMessages.0: Please review VSEPR theory, electron regions, and how lone pairs affect geometry.
